notes: Round 5 — spotlight fix: added pin-image-library step (targetSelector .aha-modal__content) so the dialog IS spotlighted when it first opens. pin-search-save step reverts to slide-plugin-iframe-config-container (always-present) for stability — the tour stalls if .aha-modal__content is used for the step that saves and closes the dialog. Hybrid design: step 6 shows correct dialog spotlight; step 7 (search+save) uses settings-panel target for tour completion.

steps.display_text: A Pin on Image slide lets participants tap anywhere on a custom image to mark their answer.
steps.tts_text: A Pin on Image slide lets participants tap anywhere on a custom image to mark their answer.

steps.display_text: Click New Presentation to start a fresh deck.
steps.tts_text: Click New Presentation to start a fresh deck.

steps.display_text: Select Pin on Image from the Unscored section of the slide type picker.
steps.tts_text: Select Pin on Image from the Unscored section of the slide type picker.

steps.display_text: Type your spatial question — tell participants exactly what to pin.
steps.tts_text: Type your spatial question — tell participants exactly what to pin.

steps.display_text: Click the image area to open the image library and choose a photo for your slide.
steps.tts_text: Click the image area to open the image library and choose a photo for your slide.

steps.display_text: Your image library is open. Switch to Stock Photos to browse millions of free, searchable images.
steps.tts_text: Your image library is open. Switch to Stock Photos to browse millions of free, searchable images.

steps.display_text: Click Stock Photos, type 'world map' in the search box, pick the first result, and hit Save to add it to your slide.
steps.tts_text: Click Stock Photos, type world map in the search box, pick the first result, and hit Save to add it to your slide.

steps.display_text: Click Preview to rehearse before going live.
steps.tts_text: Click Preview to rehearse before going live.

steps.display_text: The participant screen shows your image. Your audience taps it on their phone to drop a pin.
steps.tts_text: The participant screen shows your image. Your audience taps it on their phone to drop a pin.

steps.display_text: Pins appear live on the presenter screen as responses come in.
steps.tts_text: Pins appear live on the presenter screen as responses come in.

steps.display_text: That's all it takes. Hit Present when you're ready to go live.
steps.tts_text: That's all it takes. Hit Present when you're ready to go live.

mix_command: FFMPEG=./node_modules/ffmpeg-static/ffmpeg && $FFMPEG -y -i out/pin-on-image-r5-trimmed.webm -i out/s01.mp3 -i out/s02.mp3 -i out/s03.mp3 -i out/s04.mp3 -i out/s05-add-image.mp3 -i out/s06-image-library.mp3 -i out/s07-search-save.mp3 -i out/s08.mp3 -i out/s09.mp3 -i out/s10.mp3 -i out/s11.mp3 -filter_complex '[1:a]adelay=0|0[a1];[2:a]adelay=4500|4500[a2];[3:a]adelay=6500|6500[a3];[4:a]adelay=12000|12000[a4];[5:a]adelay=17500|17500[a5];[6:a]adelay=21500|21500[a6];[7:a]adelay=24500|24500[a7];[8:a]adelay=46500|46500[a8];[9:a]adelay=49000|49000[a9];[10:a]adelay=52000|52000[a10];[11:a]adelay=53500|53500[a11];[a1][a2][a3][a4][a5][a6][a7][a8][a9][a10][a11]amix=inputs=11:normalize=0[aout]' -map 0:v -map '[aout]' -c:v copy -c:a libopus -b:a 64k out/pin-on-image-r5-narrated.webm
